title: SOZA brings stress testing directly to UAE workplaces as regional pressures mount | HealthCare Middle East & Africa - No.1 Health & Pharma Manufacturing Industry Publication
summary: SOZA's service aims to detect early imbalances that can have an impact on sleep, immunity, cardiovascular health, and workplace productivity.
thbAction: Use the article as an entry point to pitch THB's commercial visibility layer for territory planning, account prioritization, and next-best-action for pharma leadership teams.
contentHtml:
UAE—Health technology company SOZA has launched a new on-site health data analytics and stress assessment service in the UAE, taking advanced wellness diagnostics directly into organizational premises.

The initiative removes the need for employees to visit a clinic, making it easier than ever for companies to understand how stress is affecting their teams.

The program works by collecting high volumes of physiological data within the workplace itself, measuring key indicators such as stress resilience, recovery capacity, and overall physiological balance.

Through this data, both organizations and individuals can gain a clearer picture of how their bodies are responding to physical and emotional pressures — and act on that knowledge before problems develop.

With ongoing regional developments continuing to affect daily life, stress has emerged as a growing threat to both physical and mental health across the UAE.

SOZA’s service is designed to get ahead of this challenge, identifying early imbalances that may affect sleep, immunity, cardiovascular health, and workplace productivity.

The findings can then inform targeted, data-driven interventions at both individual and group levels.

Dr. Priya Pattni-Dossani, Managing Director of SOZA DAS, says the company’s goal is to make wellness measurable and accessible, particularly at a time when so many people are navigating heightened uncertainty.

By bringing stress testing on-site, SOZA enables employees to better understand their bodies and take concrete steps toward improving resilience, performance, and quality of life.

Premal Patel, CEO of Healthy Minds Club UAE, sees the collaboration as a meaningful step forward for workplace health in the region.

Together with SOZA, his organization is bringing a data-driven approach to the market that helps companies and their teams understand how stress manifests physically — and what they can do about it.

The scalability of the program is one of its most practical advantages.

High-volume on-site testing means organizations can assess large groups efficiently, generating aggregated insights that are far more actionable than individual check-ups alone.

Rather than waiting for health issues to surface, companies can use the data to shift toward a genuinely preventative model of care.

To encourage uptake, SOZA is currently offering the service at a special rate of AED 100 (USD 27) per test for a limited time.
